title: teucrium polium subsp. rupestricola
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. rupestricola is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium polium, also known as the gypsywort or woodland germander, native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subsp. rupestricola include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to respiratory problems.
